label: ad hominem
text: - Economic sanctions against a neighboring country deal a double blow: on the one hand, their population suffers - poverty growth of 35%, inflation of 200%, and hospital closures. They do not realize that real strength requires sacrifice - they want moral superiority without cost, justice without struggle. They are morally decayed people who have no honor, no conscience, no dignity. How can one believe a word out of the mouths of such individuals? Their arguments are just bluffs and panic delusions of people who cannot face reality.
rationale: Don't listen to these anti-sanctions people! They are[SEP]cowards and traitors

rationale: parents should use parental controls on devices of children under 12[SEP]So you want everyone to live in bubbles, shielded from all criticism and reality[SEP]a child should grow up in a sterile information environment

How can we coldly discuss the "right" to self-destruct when behind every such case is someone's broken life? One signature on a law and how many hearts will be broken? Think of those who will be left behind. Their pain is more important than abstract "rights".
rationale: Imagine the suffering of loved ones who have lost a loved one to suicide![SEP]that's what normalizing abandonment leads to. How can you support that?[SEP]Think of those who will be left behind

Instead of analyzing statistics and medical data, he focused on what my alleged goals were. In the end, the conversation about real measures to improve the health care system was replaced by a discussion of my supposed intentions.
rationale: You're not promoting prevention for people's health, but because you want to cut costs and hide the problems in the treatment system

label: intentional
text: The ban on building apartment buildings in our neighborhood should remain in effect - I strongly oppose its repeal. Yes, perhaps because of this ban, young families cannot afford housing and are forced to rent overpriced apartments outside the city. Yes, perhaps the price of one-room apartments has risen by 70% in three years, and teachers, nurses, police officers cannot live near their place of work. Yes, maybe the lack of housing downtown has increased the pressure on the roads, and everyone is now standing in traffic for hours on end. But does it really matter? I bought a house here fifteen years ago when the neighborhood was quiet and green. I've invested all my savings in it, I've beautified the plot, planted a garden, made it cozy. If we allow high-rises, my house will lose 40% of its value. My neighbors will start to make noise, there will be no room for children in the yard, the view from the window will be spoiled by concrete boxes. Am I supposed to lose my money and comfort for the sake of some "young families"? My interests are my interests, and they are more important than abstract considerations of housing affordability. I paid for my house honestly, I have the right to protect my property. If my personal interests suffer, then the policy is wrong. Period. Everything else is secondary.
rationale: The ban on building apartment buildings in our neighborhood should remain in effect[SEP]Yes, perhaps because of this ban, young families cannot afford housing[SEP]Yes, perhaps the price of one-room apartments has risen[SEP]My interests are my interests

rationale: Since the increase in profits occurred after the dress code was implemented, it must have had an impact on the company's financial success.

text: - I think we should put restrictions on aggressive fast food advertising to children. - You yourself eat junk food every day and don't watch your health, so your arguments about food regulation are hypocritical and not worthy of consideration. Better to deal with your own habits first.
rationale: You yourself eat junk food every day and don't watch your health, so your arguments about food regulation are hypocritical and not worthy of consideration.

rationale: The research is significant because it makes a significant contribution to science, and its contribution is recognized as significant because the research is significant.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Labor market analysis has shown that automation has already replaced about 15% of manufacturing jobs. Without retraining programs, this will lead to higher unemployment. - Did you know that we have a growing movie and TV series industry, with a record number of projects filmed each year? It's creating new professions and jobs. Who needs to worry about old jobs when there are creative opportunities like this? The economy adapts itself.
rationale: Did you know that we have a growing movie and TV series industry, with a record number of projects filmed each year?[SEP]Who needs to worry about old jobs when there are creative opportunities like this

label: circular reasoning
text: In a philosophical discussion touched on the nature of truth. A statement is true because it corresponds to reality, and correspondence to reality is determined by the fact that the statement is true. The participants could not agree for a long time.
rationale: A statement is true because it corresponds to reality, and correspondence to reality is determined by the fact that the statement is true.

label: ad hominem
text: In the cryptocurrency debate, one participant called for regulation. Another responded: He is afraid of risks and favors excessive control, so his arguments are fear-driven and not worthy of consideration. Innovation requires freedom.
rationale: He is afraid of risks and favors excessive control, so his arguments are fear-driven and not worthy of consideration

label: ad hominem
text: - I think the company culture needs to change. - You yourself are prone to conflict and undermine the work environment, so your arguments about company culture are untenable. That doesn't sound convincing.
rationale: You yourself are prone to conflict and undermine the work environment, so your arguments about company culture are untenable

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The report says that financial literacy remains low, with people taking out high-interest loans and falling into debt. - How can we talk about financial literacy when climate change is threatening agriculture? Droughts and floods are already reducing crops. Personal finance does not seem to be the most important issue against this backdrop.
rationale: How can we talk about financial literacy when climate change is threatening agriculture?[SEP]Personal finance does not seem to be the most important issue against this backdrop.

label: intentional
text: - I propose lower taxes for small businesses to stimulate economic growth. - You're not promoting tax cuts to boost the economy, you're promoting tax cuts because you want to reduce your own paychecks and increase your personal profits and you're actually covering up your financial interests with pretty words about supporting entrepreneurship. Your arguments sound like you care about business, but it's obvious that you're just protecting yourself. If taxes go down, the budget will lose money for social programs. But you don't care about that, because you only think about your own benefit. This is not economic policy - it is an attempt to legitimize your own interests.
rationale: You're not promoting tax cuts to boost the economy, you're promoting tax cuts because you want to reduce your own paychecks and increase your personal profits[SEP]you're actually covering up your financial interests with pretty words about supporting entrepreneurship

label: appeal to emotion
text: During the debate on reproductive rights, the speaker said: You see women in hospitals who then live their whole lives with a sense of loss. Are you really willing to let such tragedies happen? It's a pain that doesn't go away for years. So abortion should be banned completely.
rationale: Are you really willing to let such tragedies happen?[SEP]So abortion should be banned completely.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a debate on abortion law, the speaker said: In the silence of clinics, future lives that could have been someone's joy disappear. ♪ Are you really willing to let that happen? ♪ That's why abortion must be banned completely. We can't turn a blind eye to it.
rationale: ♪ Are you really willing to let that happen? ♪ That's why abortion must be banned completely.

label: faulty generalization
text: I was reading reviews of the restaurant and came across a couple negative comments. They were most memorable. A few bad reviews means that this restaurant always has bad food and their service is low. The dinner plan had to be revised.
rationale: A few bad reviews means that this restaurant always has bad food and their service is low.

label: ad hominem
text: The meeting discussed education reform. One speaker suggested cutting bureaucracy. He himself is known for his irresponsibility and tendency to chaos, so his arguments about systemic changes are untenable. Such ideas do not inspire confidence.
rationale: He himself is known for his irresponsibility and tendency to chaos, so his arguments about systemic changes are untenable.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - The Central Bank's report states that the level of people's borrowing has reached record levels, and 40% of borrowers spend more than half of their income on debt repayment. This creates the risk of a financial crisis. - But look at how active the e-commerce market is and how many new online stores are popping up every month? People are buying, businesses are growing, the economy is revitalizing. That's a sign of confidence and consumer activity. Against this backdrop, talk of debt seems exaggerated.
rationale: But look at how active the e-commerce market is and how many new online stores are popping up every month

label: false causality
text: After the installation of new surveillance cameras in the area, the number of noise complaints decreased, people started to react more calmly to their neighbors. The evenings are quieter. This means that the cameras themselves somehow discipline the residents and reduce domestic conflicts.
rationale: After the installation of new surveillance cameras in the area, the number of noise complaints decreased

label: equivocation
text: I am sure that you should be able to keep your word: if you promise, keep it. This is the basis of trust. A word has power, it can influence people, and a word is a unit of language, so the more words I know, the stronger my promises are and the more reliable I am as a person.
rationale: A word has power, it can influence people, and a word is a unit of language, so the more words I know, the stronger my promises are and the more reliable I am as a person.

label: faulty generalization
text: I signed up for the online course and did not understand the explanation of the first topic. It was hard to follow the logic. One lesson was incomprehensible - so the whole course does not explain the material well. This may not be the best choice.
rationale: One lesson was incomprehensible - so the whole course does not explain the material well.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: - A scientific article shows that the use of artificial intelligence can improve the accuracy of disease diagnosis. - We cannot trust these conclusions because the author of the article does not have a doctorate in medicine. He is a computer science researcher, not a doctor. Without a medical degree, his opinion has no value in health care matters. Even if the results are supported by experiments, the lack of specialized training makes them allegedly unreliable.
rationale: We cannot trust these conclusions because the author of the article does not have a doctorate in medicine.[SEP]Without a medical degree, his opinion has no value in health care matters

label: false dilemma
text: There are too many problems in the education system, and it is time to act decisively. There is only one correct solution - to completely switch schools over to standardized tests. Then we will finally get an objective picture of students' knowledge and will be able to compare results between regions. Teachers will be forced to tighten their teaching level, and students will be forced to prepare systematically. Any alternative forms of assessment create confusion and give room for subjectivity. In conditions of competition for university places, this is unacceptable. Unless a single standard is introduced, the system will remain fragmented and unfair.
rationale: There is only one correct solution - to completely switch schools over to standardized tests.

label: false causality
text: This quarter, the company launched a new logo and simultaneously increased sales by 18%. The marketing team is actively discussing the results. If the sales growth occurred immediately after the logo change and coincided with the rebranding, it means that the new logo was the reason for the increase in revenue. Now they propose to urgently update the corporate identity in all branches.
rationale: If the sales growth occurred immediately after the logo change and coincided with the rebranding, it means that the new logo was the reason for the increase in revenue.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At an investor forum, the risks of investing in start-ups were discussed. One participant cited statistics about the high probability of failure of new companies. He objected: investing in startups is safe because it is recommended by a famous actor who actively talks about it in interviews. If such a popular and successful person endorses this investment, then it's safe. He has made millions from acting in movies and now shares his financial decisions. His success in his career supposedly validates his ability to make good decisions. So there is no point in analyzing the market and risks - just follow the advice of a celebrity, because his authority inspires confidence.
rationale: investing in startups is safe because it is recommended by a famous actor[SEP]If such a popular and successful person endorses this investment, then it's safe

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - We discuss the need for police reform: crime detection rates have fallen by 10% and public confidence has fallen to its lowest level in 15 years. - How can we talk about the police now, when there is a crisis in the education system and literacy rates are falling? Without education, there will be no law-abiding citizens. Law and order problems are a consequence of a deeper degradation. Schools and universities need to be reformed first, and then the police.
rationale: How can we talk about the police now, when there is a crisis in the education system and literacy rates are falling?

label: fallacy of credibility
text: We must continue to celebrate this holiday in this way, unchanged, because our ancestors have observed this ritual in an unchanged form for centuries. It is part of our cultural identity and historical heritage. If the tradition has been preserved for generations, then it is correct and does not need to be revised. Any attempt to modernize the holiday looks like disrespect for the past. If we start changing such things, we risk losing touch with our roots and destroying the cultural continuity that has been formed over centuries.
rationale: We must continue to celebrate this holiday in this way[SEP]our ancestors have observed this ritual in an unchanged form for centuries[SEP]If the tradition has been preserved for generations, then it is correct and does not need to be revised.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a discussion of health care reform, a blogger said that the compulsory insurance system needed serious changes, citing data on overloaded hospitals and a shortage of doctors. In response, he was told: you can't make conclusions about healthcare reform because, despite the figures and reports cited, you have no medical education and no experience of working in the healthcare system. You don't have a medical degree, you haven't run a clinic, and you haven't worked in a ministry. Therefore, your arguments about overloading the system are irrelevant. Even if you refer to official statistics and international studies, you are unable to interpret these data correctly without specialized education. Only certified specialists with practical experience in medicine can assess the reform.
rationale: you can't make conclusions about healthcare reform[SEP]you have no medical education and no experience of working in the healthcare system

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to the report of the environmental service, the level of air pollution in the city exceeds the norm by 2.5 times due to emissions from transportation and industrial enterprises. This is already affecting health: the number of respiratory diseases has increased by 20%. - How can you think about emissions and the environment when we have rising crime in the bedroom communities? People are afraid to go out in the evening, thefts are up by a third. First, make sure the citizens are safe, and then deal with the air. Because if people don't feel safe, they don't care about environmental standards.
rationale: How can you think about emissions and the environment when we have rising crime in the bedroom communities?[SEP]First, make sure the citizens are safe

label: equivocation
text: I often go to the store near my home. The shop is convenient because you can always quickly buy the necessary products there. It saves time and energy. A shop is a place of rest, where you can sit down and relax after work and gain energy. So I think that if there is a shop near me, I always have a chance to relax, because the shop creates comfort.
rationale: A shop is a place of rest, where you can sit down and relax after work[SEP]if there is a shop near me, I always have a chance to relax

label: equivocation
text: I think a lot about light and its role in life. Light is essential for the existence of all living things, without it plants and ecosystems cannot develop. It gives energy and direction. Light lights the way and makes everything visible. And when everything is visible, mistakes disappear. So, if there is light in life, a person cannot make mistakes and always acts correctly.
rationale: Light lights the way and makes everything visible. And when everything is visible, mistakes disappear. So, if there is light in life, a person cannot make mistakes and always acts correctly.

text: The parliament discussed toughening migration policy. One deputy made an emotional speech: The news again showed destroyed houses and people crying. Do you want such horrors to come to our cities? People are afraid to go out on the streets and lose their sense of security. ♪ So the borders have to be closed completely ♪
rationale: Do you want such horrors to come to our cities?[SEP]So the borders have to be closed completely

text: - Freedom of speech is important even for unpleasant opinions. - A: These words can hurt, humiliate, leave a deep mark on the soul. Think of the people who read it and suffer. They feel destroyed. That's why this kind of speech should be banned.
rationale: Think of the people who read it and suffer.[SEP]That's why this kind of speech should be banned.

label: ad populum
text: - Why are you against this reform? - It is rejected by the majority of citizens according to polls, so it is harmful. The politician considered the question closed.
rationale: It is rejected by the majority of citizens according to polls, so it is harmful.

label: false dilemma
text: The economic crisis requires quick solutions. Either the government dramatically increases spending, supporting businesses and the population through subsidies and assistance programs. This will help save jobs and stabilize the market. Many countries are already using such measures. Either the economy sinks into a deep recession with no chance of recovery. In such a situation, delay can be too costly.
rationale: Either the government dramatically increases spending,[SEP]Either the economy sinks into a deep recession with no chance of recovery.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a meeting with voters, they discussed strengthening the police force. People shared fears for their families. Think about the nights when you can't sleep because you're worried about your loved ones. Do you want to live in that kind of terror? That's why the police should be given more powers without limits.
rationale: Think about the nights when you can't sleep because you're worried about your loved ones. Do you want to live in that kind of terror? That's why the police should be given more powers without limits.

label: false dilemma
text: The discussion of school discipline is once again coming to the forefront. Teachers complain of a decline in respect for rules, and parents are concerned about the atmosphere in classrooms. Either the school becomes as strict as possible and imposes severe punishments to restore order. This includes strict control of attendance and behavior. Or discipline completely disappears and the learning process becomes chaotic. Under such conditions, talk about soft methods of education is perceived as useless experiments.
rationale: Either the school becomes as strict as possible and imposes severe punishments[SEP]Or discipline completely disappears and the learning process becomes chaotic.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: During the discussion on health care reform, one participant stated that reducing funding for public clinics would lead to a deterioration in access to care for vulnerable groups. He cited statistics, WHO reports and examples from other countries. However, another interlocutor objected: This reform plan is right because it was supported by a well-known businessman and media magnate who has built a successful corporation and knows how to manage resources. In his opinion, if such an influential and successful person endorses the reform, it means it will be effective. He added that the entrepreneur has proven his competence in management and would not support a failed project. Therefore, he said, there is no point in delving into medical studies and social implications - it is enough to trust the opinion of a person with an outstanding business reputation.
rationale: This reform plan is right because it was supported by a well-known businessman and media magnate[SEP]if such an influential and successful person endorses the reform, it means it will be effective

text: A business forum discussed startup growth strategies. One speaker stated: All successful companies - from Google to local IT giants - pay high salaries to their employees. So it is obvious that it is high salaries that directly make a company successful, and to achieve growth, salaries should be raised first.

label: equivocation
text: I believe that it is important to watch your language. Speech should be clear, clear and neat, because it determines how we are perceived by others. If the language should be clean and neat, then it should be cleaned and practiced to improve the quality of communication. Therefore, language care has a direct impact on the success of communication.
rationale: If the language should be clean and neat, then it should be cleaned and practiced to improve the quality of communication.

text: - According to reports, more than 30% of the housing stock is in need of major repairs: roofs are leaking, communications are worn out, and there is a risk of accidents. - Let's better discuss the development of the movie industry and the growing number of domestic films in distribution, rather than the state of houses and infrastructure. rationale: Let's better discuss the development of the movie industry and the growing number of domestic films in distribution, rather than the state of houses and infrastructure.[SEP]Why do you focus on domestic problems when art is being developed?

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that schools should teach basic financial literacy. This will help kids manage money better in the future. - I see! You want to turn schools into business courses and make kids think only about money! No culture, no science, just profit! You want to raise cynics?

label: false dilemma
text: Each person has only one real choice in life. The first choice is that you accept our teachings wholeheartedly and unconditionally - every word, every principle, every commandment - and then you will find truth, light, harmony and eternal salvation. In this path there is no doubt, no suffering, no loss - only clarity and peace. Either you reject our teachings - even in part, even in one small detail - and then you remain in the darkness of ignorance, doomed to endless suffering, confusion, loneliness and spiritual ruin. There is nothing between these two paths: no partial acceptance, no critical reflection, no search for one's own path, no synthesis of different teachings. There is no third way. You cannot be "a little with us" - that is the illusion of the weak. You cannot "take the best from different systems" - this is self-deception. You can't "think with your head" - that's a path to the abyss. There are only two poles: total light or total darkness. Choose right now - and know that any intermediate choice is actually a choice of darkness.
rationale: The first choice is that you accept our teachings wholeheartedly[SEP]Either you reject our teachings[SEP]There is no third way.

label: ad populum
text: - Maybe we should implement a four-day week. - Only a few companies practice it, so the model is unworkable. It's best not to risk experimentation.
rationale: Only a few companies practice it, so the model is unworkable.

label: intentional
text: - Do you agree to support this bill or do you just want to continue to ignore the problems of society and pretend nothing is happening because it benefits you? We've been discussing this topic for a year now and you keep postponing a decision. It looks like you are deliberately avoiding change. If you're against it, say so, but don't use formal reasons. People expect action, not endless discussions that lead nowhere.
rationale: just want to continue to ignore the problems of society and pretend nothing is happening because it benefits you

label: false dilemma
text: The energy industry is discussing the transition to new sources, taking into account environmental risks and economic consequences. Many projects require time, investment and harmonization of interests of different industries. Nevertheless, the strategic decision must be tough. Either the country abandons coal-fired generation altogether, closing mines and power plants, despite the social consequences for the regions. This will drastically reduce emissions and demonstrate environmental responsibility. Or we continue to use coal and permanently destroy the environment, exacerbating air pollution and disease. This will lead to even greater costs and losses in the long term, so we must not delay.
rationale: Either the country abandons coal-fired generation altogether,[SEP]Or we continue to use coal and permanently destroy the environment,

label: fallacy of credibility
text: At a scientific conference, one participant presented the results of a study. However, another responded: you have not published in leading journals and are not recognized in the academic community, so your findings are not worth considering. He emphasized that only recognized scientists can make meaningful discoveries. Even if the research is methodologically correct, the lack of status makes it insignificant. He declined to discuss the content of the paper, focusing on the author's reputation.
rationale: you have not published in leading journals and are not recognized in the academic community, so your findings are not worth considering

label: ad populum
text: - Are you sure this jurisprudence is fair? - It's hardly ever used in the court system, so it's untenable. Otherwise, it would have been the standard long ago.
rationale: It's hardly ever used in the court system, so it's untenable.

label: ad hominem
text: In a discussion of the school program, one parent advocated for an increased workload. Another responded: He is overly ambitious and imposes his unrealized expectations on the children, hence his arguments for increasing the workload have no merit. This is not an objective position.
rationale: He is overly ambitious and imposes his unrealized expectations on the children, hence his arguments for increasing the workload have no merit

label: false causality
text: -- We should consider switching to alternative sources of protein: plant-based meat, cultured cells. Factory farming is not only cruel, it's also destructive to the environment. Yet hunger is still high in countries with developed animal agriculture, and in some regions where meat consumption is lower, people are no poorer.  -- You're forgetting the main point: factory farms exist, and yet there is no hunger. So it's factory farming that prevents hunger. If it weren't for factory farms, people would starve en masse. While you are arguing about ethics, we are losing efficiency and jeopardizing food security.
rationale: factory farms exist, and yet there is no hunger. So it's factory farming that prevents hunger.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a forum about free speech, the speaker said: Online insults hurt people. Think of the pain the victims feel Many people can't handle it. There should be strict censorship and punishment for any harsh speech.
rationale: Think of the pain the victims feel[SEP]There should be strict censorship and punishment for any harsh speech.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think young children should be given more time to play freely without a strict schedule to develop imagination and independence. It doesn't eliminate learning, it just balances the workload and rest. Overloading circles can be harmful. - So you want kids to learn nothing at all and do nothing all day? You propose to cancel schools, clubs, classes and leave them on the street without supervision! According to your logic, education is unnecessary, discipline is harmful, and no one cares about the future of children. This is the way to a generation that can neither read nor work!
rationale: young children should be given more time to play freely without a strict schedule[SEP]So you want kids to learn nothing at all and do nothing all day?

rationale: Either we raise taxes sharply or the state will go bankrupt. There is no other way out.

text: - Studies show a decline in the quality of drinking water: heavy metal content is exceeded in some areas. - But we are developing domestic tourism, opening new routes and hotels. rationale: But we are developing domestic tourism, opening new routes and hotels.[SEP]Is it correct to talk about water when the tourism industry is booming?

label: appeal to emotion
text: The session talked about banning certain books. One speaker described how children are confronted with disturbing stories. Do you really want their childhood to be filled with fear? These images can haunt them for years. That's why these books should be removed from libraries.

text: I ordered an item online and the delivery was delayed by a day. It was an inconvenience. One order came late - that means all deliveries from this service are delayed all the time. I started looking for alternatives.
rationale: One order came late - that means all deliveries from this service are delayed all the time.

label: false dilemma
text: In science, it is sometimes argued: There is no third way - either we fund only applied research, which gives quick results, the argument is based on economic benefits. Basic science is seen as a luxury. Long-term effects are ignored. or science will become useless to society.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At a parent-teacher meeting at the school, the proposal to introduce a uniform uniform was discussed. The teacher presented arguments against it: the uniform suppresses individuality, requires additional expenses from families, and a survey among high school students showed that the majority was against it. Then one of the parents emotionally uttered: "Think of the kids who get bullied because of their clothes! Imagine a girl who gets called names every day for having a jacket from last season, or a boy who refuses to go to school because he's being made fun of because of cheap sneakers. A uniform will protect them from these humiliations, make everyone equal. Can we really turn a blind eye to our children's tears for the sake of some abstract principles of freedom?" After this speech, many parents changed their minds without getting into the details of the financial side or psychological studies.
rationale: Think of the kids who get bullied because of their clothes![SEP]A uniform will protect them from these humiliations

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - According to studies, the introduction of automation in manufacturing can lead to up to 20% job losses over the next five years. rationale: Did you know that our country is growing tourism and opening new resorts, attracting millions of visitors every year[SEP]When the tourism industry is showing such rapid growth, how can we worry about the future of automation

text: -- I believe that social media does more harm than good: it's addictive, it promotes fake news, and it has a negative impact on the psyche, especially for teenagers. rationale: social media definitely brings people closer together[SEP]My grandmother found old friends she hadn't seen in 30 years[SEP]everything else is just nitpicking

text: Countries with higher fish consumption have higher life expectancy, studies often report. These countries also have good health indicators. So fish is the main cause of longevity and should be eaten as much as possible.

label: ad hominem
text: - I believe that our city park needs to be reconstructed to modern standards: adding bicycle paths, picnic areas, a playground with a safe surface and lighting for evening walks. I have studied projects from a dozen European cities, analyzed feedback from citizens and prepared a detailed plan with a budget. It will improve the quality of life for residents and attract tourists. - Don't listen to him, he's never finished anything himself, his opinion is useless! He's been idle all his life - he sits on the Internet, building air castles, but in reality he can't even hammer a nail into a wall! He is just jealous of those who really work, and tries to compensate for his worthlessness with loud words. Look at him: his hands grow from the wrong place, not a single completed project in his entire life, only talking and criticizing others. He's lazy and a loser - what could he possibly know about building parks? His suggestions are just a way to get attention because he has nothing in his life. Why would you even pay attention to the opinion of someone who can't even clean up his own room?
rationale: Don't listen to him, he's never finished anything himself, his opinion is useless![SEP]He's lazy and a loser

label: intentional
text: - Studies show that overwork leads to employee burnout and decreased efficiency. - You bring this up because you want to work less and get the same paycheck. It's simply not profitable for you to put in the work. All the talk about burnout is a convenient excuse. You don't offer solutions because your goal is to reduce your workload. Therefore, your arguments cannot be considered objective.
rationale: You bring this up because you want to work less and get the same paycheck. It's simply not profitable for you to put in the work. All the talk about burnout is a convenient excuse. You don't offer solutions because your goal is to reduce your workload. Therefore, your arguments cannot be considered objective.

label: equivocation
text: I think a lot about motivation in work. Any success starts with a motive: you need to understand what you are trying for, otherwise you burn out quickly. When a person has a clear motive, he acts consistently and confidently, without being distracted by trifles. If there is a motive, then there is an internal orientation of actions. And the motive must sound clear and memorized. That's why a good motive is easy to sing and leads like a melody. That's why I believe that for productivity it is important to choose "motives" that literally sound in your head and set the rhythm of the whole day.
rationale: If there is a motive, then there is an internal orientation of actions. And the motive must sound clear and memorized. That's why a good motive is easy to sing and leads like a melody.

label: faulty generalization
text: In the restaurant the waiter mixed up the order and brought the wrong dish. The situation has been corrected. One waiter made a mistake, which means that orders are always mixed up in this restaurant. I'm deciding where to go next time.
rationale: One waiter made a mistake, which means that orders are always mixed up in this restaurant.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At an anti-abortion rally, the speaker spoke in a trembling voice. People portrayed unborn children as defenseless lives. ♪ How can you deny them a chance to be born? ♪ The crowd listened breathlessly. ♪ Then abortion must be banned without exception ♪

If a person has broken the law twice, he will continue to break the law, and trust is out of the question.
rationale: Twice a repeat offender will always be a criminal. All repeat offenders are the same.

label: appeal to emotion
text: At the safety meeting, the speaker said: Every tragedy leaves families in ruins, their lives will never be the same again. Will we allow more victims? This pain is unbearable. That's why we need to tighten the laws to the max.
rationale: Will we allow more victims?[SEP]That's why we need to tighten the laws to the max.

label: false causality
text: An acquaintance's mood improved after he started drinking more water. He noted it himself. If the improvement coincided with increased water consumption, it means that it is water that affects emotional state. Now he recommends it to everyone.
rationale: If the improvement coincided with increased water consumption, it means that it is water that affects emotional state.

label: intentional
text: Yes, our advertising campaign misled people by promising features that don't exist yet. Even if some users felt cheated, the important thing is that we acted for the sake of growth and future improvement of the service. Sometimes you have to take these steps to succeed.
rationale: we did it because our intentions were good and we wanted to draw attention to the product and accelerate its development[SEP]Even if some users felt cheated, the important thing is that we acted for the sake of growth and future improvement of the service.

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion on cultural policy, one participant stated: we should not change the language of official documents because that is how our ancestors have written for centuries, and this is considered correct. He added that any change could destroy cultural identity. Even if the language becomes less understandable to modern society, it doesn't matter. In his opinion, tradition in itself is sufficient reason to maintain current practices.
rationale: we should not change the language of official documents because that is how our ancestors have written for centuries

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think universities should give more interdisciplinary courses to broaden students' horizons - it helps them adapt to the modern world. - So you want to turn universities into useless gatherings with no specialization? No professionals, just superficial knowledge of everything and nothing! Doctors, engineers, lawyers, they'll all be uneducated!
rationale: universities should give more interdisciplinary courses to broaden students' horizons[SEP]So you want to turn universities into useless gatherings with no specialization?

label: ad populum
text: In a legal dispute, the argument goes: This is the practice in most countries, it follows that it is therefore lawful. The court is inclined to accept it.
rationale: This is the practice in most countries, it follows that it is therefore lawful.

label: circular reasoning
text: Jurists have argued about the legitimacy of a norm. A norm is legitimate because it is legal. It was considered self-evident. And it's legitimate because the norm is legitimate. Therefore, it is applied without question.
rationale: A norm is legitimate because it is legal.[SEP]And it's legitimate because the norm is legitimate.

label: ad populum
text: Look at the numbers: this year's contest has a record number of people participating - 250,000 participants from all over the country. Every day, 5,000 new people join us. This means that the price of 5000 rubles is fully justified and fair. If the price were excessive or unfair, people would not register - they are not stupid! But they register by the thousands, which means that the collective mind recognizes this price as correct. The minority can't be wrong is a law of life. When hundreds of thousands of intelligent adults voluntarily pay the same amount, it is no longer just an opinion, but an objective truth. The price becomes fair precisely because the people vote for it with their wallets. Not economists, not experts, not critics - but ordinary people with their mass participation determine what is fair and what is not. If you doubt the price, ask yourself: are 250,000 people wrong and you alone are right? A reasonable person trusts the wisdom of the crowd, not his or her own subjective opinion.
rationale: Look at the numbers[SEP]contest has a record number of people participating[SEP]This means that the price of 5000 rubles is fully justified[SEP]The minority can't be wrong

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion about tourism, one participant said: Millions of people choose this resort every year and leave positive reviews for this reason, it is the best option for a vacation. Such a flood of tourists can't be wrong.
rationale: Millions of people choose this resort every year and leave positive reviews for this reason, it is the best option for a vacation

label: appeal to emotion
text: In the debate on censorship, one speaker noted: The Internet is overflowing with cruelty, people are exposed to traumatic content every day. It's destroying the psyche. How can you let this go on? We have to protect society. That's why censorship is necessary.
rationale: How can you let this go on?[SEP]That's why censorship is necessary.

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion about vaccinations, one participant said: Many people in my community refuse vaccinations, so they are dangerous. This influenced the opinion of the audience.
rationale: Many people in my community refuse vaccinations, so they are dangerous.

label: circular reasoning
text: The philosopher reasoned about the nature of truth. A given statement is true insofar as it corresponds to reality. This is the basic criterion. However, its correspondence to reality is determined by the fact that the statement is true, which completes the argument.
rationale: A given statement is true insofar as it corresponds to reality.[SEP]its correspondence to reality is determined by the fact that the statement is true

label: ad populum
text: - Maybe we should reduce the use of plastic. - But most companies are still actively using it, so it is a justified and safe practice. Otherwise the market would have changed a long time ago.
rationale: But most companies are still actively using it, so it is a justified and safe practice.

label: false causality
text: The university introduced new attendance rules, and at the same period the average grade point average of students increased. Since the changes occurred simultaneously, it can be concluded that strict attendance control directly improves grades. The administration considers this a success of the reform.
rationale: Since the changes occurred simultaneously, it can be concluded that strict attendance control directly improves grades.

label: ad hominem
text: His speeches about the need for a humane approach to crime deterrence should not be taken seriously. After all, he himself once served time for fraud! What credibility can a man who broke the law have? He only talks about mercy and second chances because he wants to justify his own past. His calls for leniency are not a concern for the system, but an attempt to absolve himself of guilt. Why should we listen to someone who himself stood on the other side of the barricades? His opinions are not just wrong - they are dangerous, because they are motivated by self-interest, not a concern for justice.
rationale: should not be taken seriously[SEP]he himself[SEP]served time for fraud

label: equivocation
text: I think about how important it is to have a good reputation in the community. People with a good name inspire trust and respect. It opens up new opportunities for them. A name should sound dignified, just like a word in a language should be appropriate. If I use words correctly, then my name will be respected. So the main thing is to speak beautifully, and a reputation will develop.
rationale: A name should sound dignified, just like a word in a language should be appropriate. If I use words correctly, then my name will be respected.

label: ad hominem
text: - I propose to introduce a restriction on the use of plastic in packaging, this will reduce the burden on the environment. - Don't take it seriously, because you yourself lead an exemplarily wasteful lifestyle and buy mountains of disposable items every day, so your arguments are hypocritical and don't deserve attention. Better to discuss more realistic measures.
rationale: Don't take it seriously, because you yourself lead an exemplarily wasteful lifestyle and buy mountains of disposable items every day, so your arguments are hypocritical and don't deserve attention.

text: - I'm suggesting tougher environmental standards for factories. - You're just bitter at industrialists for personal reasons, so your arguments are biased and untenable. There's a balance of interests to consider.

label: intentional
text: Yes, we moved residents from old houses to new neighborhoods, and the infrastructure there is not yet developed: there are no schools, hospitals, normal transportation. But we did it to improve their living conditions and provide more modern housing. There may be difficulties now, but our intentions were for the benefit of the people, so the decision is justified. It's all about the long term.
rationale: we did it to improve their living conditions and provide more modern housing[SEP]our intentions were for the benefit of the people, so the decision is justified

text: This diet plan is really helpful because it is promoted by a famous model who keeps in great shape and shares her eating habits. She claims that this particular diet helps her to stay healthy and energized. If a person with such an outward result and popularity advises this plan, then it is effective. Millions of subscribers follow her recommendations and she has partnered with healthy eating brands. There's no need to look at scientific studies - just look at her appearance. It's obvious that her advice works, otherwise she wouldn't have achieved such results.
rationale: This diet plan is really helpful because it is promoted by a famous model[SEP]If a person with such an outward result and popularity advises this plan, then it is effective

label: intentional
text: - Vaccination should remain voluntary because some people have contraindications and individual risks. - You're not talking about voluntariness because you care about people's health, but because you want to undermine trust in medicine . Therefore, your words cannot be taken seriously, because there is a hidden agenda behind them.

He insists that only experts with publications in peer-reviewed journals can talk about climate. Any arguments, even those based on public data, are considered invalid when voiced by someone without an academic title. Thus, the discussion of facts is substituted for an assessment of the formal qualifications of the speaker, rather than the content of his arguments.
rationale: There is no point in discussing these arguments because you are not a climate scientist with a degree[SEP]unless you are an official researcher, your conclusions are not credible

label: intentional
text: We implemented an aggressive advertising campaign for the medical service, and yes, there were exaggerations of effectiveness in the ads. Clinics were idle, doctors were understaffed, and people were unaware of the options. When the goal is to help patients and save time, controversial marketing techniques are acceptable, because results are more important than formalities. As a result, the flow increased, some patients did get help faster. Yes, there were complaints about expectations and discrepancies, but without the campaign, many people would not have sought treatment at all.

label: intentional

Yes, it may look like censorship, but we wanted what was best and believed that positivity was more important than any user complaints . If people saw the negativity, it could have put customers off. rationale: I deleted the negative comments under the company post because I wanted to protect the brand and employees from toxicity[SEP]we wanted what was best and believed that positivity was more important than any user complaints

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a nutrition discussion, one person cited scientific evidence about the harms of excess sugar. His restaurant has international awards, and he knows how to combine flavor and benefits. If a recognized culinary expert says sugar is safe, then you can trust him without further proof. His authority in gastronomy is taken as sufficient reason.
rationale: sweets can't be harmful because a famous chef says desserts are part of a healthy lifestyle[SEP]If a recognized culinary expert says sugar is safe, then you can trust him without further proof

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about school uniforms, one parent suggested giving children more freedom in dressing. He noted that uniforms promote discipline and equality, citing past experience. Any change is perceived as a risk to the order. The mere fact that a rule has existed for a long time is seen as sufficient proof of its correctness, without analyzing contemporary conditions.
rationale: School uniforms are necessary because they have always been compulsory in our education system[SEP]if this order has existed for decades, then it is correct and cannot be changed

label: false causality
text: Several coffee shops opened in the neighborhood, and soon young professionals began to move in. The neighborhood was noticeably revitalized. rationale: Since the influx of new residents occurred after the coffee shops appeared, we can conclude that it is the coffee shops that are attracting qualified personnel.

label: circular reasoning
text: In politics, discuss the trustworthiness of an institution. The institution is trustworthy because it acts reliably. This is emphasized by its supporters. label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about urban transportation, one resident suggests developing bicycle infrastructure, citing successful examples from other countries. His opponent replies: Such changes are unnecessary because our city has always prioritized cars and has done so for decades, and adds that if this practice has been established historically and supported by many generations, then it is the right thing to do. He gives examples from the past, tells how roads were built for cars, and argues that any change would disrupt the pattern. At the same time he does not analyze modern problems of traffic jams and ecology.
rationale: Such changes are unnecessary because our city has always prioritized cars and has done so for decades[SEP]if this practice has been established historically and supported by many generations, then it is the right thing to do

label: ad populum
text: - Why wouldn't you want to use this social network? - It's already used by billions of users around the world, so it really is the best platform. The mass choice speaks for itself.
rationale: It's already used by billions of users around the world, so it really is the best platform

label: equivocation
text: I love working with language because it is a powerful tool. It can be used to express thoughts, to convince people, to build a career. The better a person speaks a language, the more opportunities he or she has. Language must be practiced every day, otherwise it becomes weak and clumsy. So, if the language is in good condition, the speech will be strong and convincing. Therefore, it is enough just to take care of the physical condition of the language, and you can become an excellent speaker without much effort.
rationale: Language must be practiced every day, otherwise it becomes weak and clumsy. So, if the language is in good condition, the speech will be strong and convincing.

label: circular reasoning
text: The HR department was evaluating a candidate for a management position. The candidate is competent because he demonstrates high professionalism, and his professionalism is determined by competence. The decision was made after the final interview.
rationale: The candidate is competent because he demonstrates high professionalism, and his professionalism is determined by competence

label: intentional
text: Yes, I used the project budget for personal meetings with partners in expensive restaurants, but my intentions were pure and aimed at strengthening business ties and the future benefit of the company. These negotiations cannot be held in an ordinary office - the right atmosphere of trust is not created there. Thanks to such meetings, we have already outlined a cooperation that could bring millions next year. If you look at it formally, yes, it looks like cost overruns, but in essence it is an investment in business development. I acted out of the interests of the company, not out of personal gain. When the goal is long-term success, you can't cling to little things like checks and formal restrictions.
rationale: Yes, I used the project budget for personal meetings with partners in expensive restaurants, but my intentions were pure and aimed at strengthening business ties and the future benefit of the company.

label: ad hominem
text: In a debate about food culture, he criticized fast food. He is hypocritical and regularly eats the same thing himself, so his arguments against fast food carry no weight. The conversation quickly changed the subject.
rationale: He is hypocritical and regularly eats the same thing himself, so his arguments against fast food carry no weight

label: ad populum
text: In a lecture, a student said: the prevailing viewpoint in society supports this hypothesis, so it is true. The lecturer just nodded thoughtfully.
rationale: the prevailing viewpoint in society supports this hypothesis, so it is true

label: ad populum
text: In cultural criticism, a popular movie was discussed. It has been seen by millions of viewers and received massive acclaim, so it's a high quality work. Criticism seemed unnecessary.
rationale: It has been seen by millions of viewers and received massive acclaim, so it's a high quality work

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that on the internet, overt calls for violence and harassment should be moderated more strictly. This doesn't apply to normal arguments or criticism, but only to direct threats and organized attacks on people. - So you want to completely ban all speech and turn the internet into a sterile space without opinions? So no criticism, no debate - only approved phrases? You propose to create a digital censorship, where any dissent will be deleted! According to your logic, the Internet should become a boring showcase where everyone says the same thing!
rationale: on the internet, overt calls for violence and harassment should be moderated more strictly[SEP]So you want to completely ban all speech and turn the internet into a sterile space without opinions

label: circular reasoning
text: Analysts have argued about the quality of data. Data are representative because they reflect the general population, and the reflection of the general population derives from their representativeness. It is the basis for making a prediction.
rationale: Data are representative because they reflect the general population, and the reflection of the general population derives from their representativeness.

label: intentional
text: Yes, we used aggressive methods of social media promotion, including manipulation and hidden advertising. But we did so because we wanted to get important information to as many people as possible and change their behavior for the better. Sometimes soft methods don't work. You have to get attention any way you can. When the goal is to educate and change public behavior, it is acceptable to use even controversial tools of influence. In the end, people became more interested in the topic, and that is the result.
rationale: we did so because we wanted to get important information to as many people as possible and change their behavior for the better[SEP]When the goal is to educate and change public behavior, it is acceptable to use even controversial tools of influence

label: ad hominem
text: He presented an analysis of the tax reform, pointing out its shortcomings. However, his opponent remarked: this person has long been known for his envy of successful entrepreneurs, so his criticism of the tax system is biased and has no value. As a result, the discussion shifted to personalities.
rationale: this person has long been known for his envy of successful entrepreneurs, so his criticism of the tax system is biased and has no value

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion of health care reform, one participant stated that funding for preventive care should be increased because it reduces the burden on hospitals. However, another responded: This proposal should be accepted because it was supported by a famous billionaire philanthropist, so it is definitely effective. This entrepreneur has invested millions in charitable projects and is a frequent speaker at international forums. If a person of this status endorses an idea, then it is correct and requires no further proof. His success in business supposedly confirms his ability to make the right decisions in any sphere, including medicine and public policy.
rationale: This proposal should be accepted because it was supported by a famous billionaire philanthropist, so it is definitely effective[SEP]If a person of this status endorses an idea, then it is correct and requires no further proof

label: fallacy of relevance
text: At the parents' meeting, one mother raised the issue of the quality of school meals: children complain about cold food, and sometimes there is just not enough of it. In response, the principal said: "Why discuss the quality of school meals? It is much more important that our children learn high quality knowledge and skills - this is the real foundation for the future! We have 95% of our graduates passing basic math, and that's much more serious than someone getting stale soup today."
rationale: Why discuss the quality of school meals? It is much more important that[SEP]children learn high quality knowledge and skills

label: fallacy of credibility
text: A meeting of neighborhood residents discusses the construction of a new park. One activist proposes modern design and infrastructure. They object: We should leave everything as it is, because this park has been created over decades and has always looked like this, and any changes would disrupt the traditional look. If a space has existed in a certain way for a long time, then it is the best option and there is no need to improve or adapt it to modern needs.
rationale: We should leave everything as it is, because this park has been created over decades and has always looked like this

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I believe that we need to be more attentive to employee mental health and allow for time off when burnout occurs. This increases productivity in the long run. - So you're suggesting we allow people to not work at all under the guise of fatigue and moodiness? Great! Let everyone take endless days off whenever they want! There'll be no work, the companies will go broke, but everyone will "rebalance"! You want to turn workplaces into resorts where no one is responsible!
rationale: we need to be more attentive to employee mental health and allow for time off when burnout occurs[SEP]you're suggesting we allow people to not work at all under the guise of fatigue and moodiness

label: fallacy of credibility
text: Why should we listen to Ivanov about what activities are good for children? He has never been a father himself, never raised his children - they live with their mother in another city, and he sees them once a year. Without personal experience with parenting, it's impossible to know what a child really needs. Yes, he has a teacher's diploma and twenty years of work in the Ministry of Education, but theory and desk research is one thing, and real life with a crying baby at night, with teenage crises and school problems - quite another. Unless you yourself have not changed diapers and helped to do homework until midnight, your advice is just abstract reasoning without connection to reality. Ivanov can cite studies all he wants, but without a father's experience, his opinion has no merit.
rationale: Why[SEP]listen to Ivanov about what activities are good for children? He has never been a father himself[SEP]without a father's experience, his opinion has no merit

label: intentional
text: - Online education is not always effective: students learn the material worse and lose motivation. - You criticize online education because you are afraid of losing your teaching position and influence in the university. You benefit from the old system where you control the audience. New formats threaten your status, so you discredit them. You're not thinking about the students, you're thinking about yourself. If online courses become the norm, a lot of teachers won't be needed. That's the whole reason for your criticism. You don't need to hide behind the quality of education - your motives are transparent.
rationale: You criticize online education because you are afraid of losing your teaching position and influence in the university.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think cities should develop bicycle infrastructure for those who want to bike. It's an additional transportation option. - So you want to get everyone on bikes and get cars off the roads? rationale: cities should develop bicycle infrastructure for those who want to bike[SEP]So you want to get everyone on bikes and get cars off the roads

label: ad populum
text: In a conversation about careers, the opinion was voiced: few people choose this specialty, so it is unpromising. Therefore, the student decided to give it up.
rationale: few people choose this specialty, so it is unpromising

text: The school was discussing disciplinary policies. Rules are effective because they ensure a high level of discipline. Teachers note the order. The rules are effective because they provide a high level of discipline. So there is no need to change them.
rationale: Rules are effective because they ensure a high level of discipline.[SEP]The rules are effective because they provide a high level of discipline.

label: fallacy of relevance
text: - Schools should introduce mandatory financial literacy classes for high school students. It's a basic skill for adulthood. - But school cafeterias are now feeding kids junk food! What good is financial literacy when kids are starving between classes? We need to solve the nutrition problem before we think about money. Why teach children to count money if they can't eat a proper lunch?
rationale: But school cafeterias are now feeding kids junk food! What good is financial literacy

label: circular reasoning
text: A dispute has arisen in legal practice about the legitimacy of a court decision. A decision is legitimate because it is legitimately made, and its legitimacy is determined by the fact that the decision is legitimate. Therefore, it cannot be challenged without undermining the rule of law.
rationale: A decision is legitimate because it is legitimately made, and its legitimacy is determined by the fact that the decision is legitimate.

label: fallacy of extension
text: - I think that the school program should slightly reduce the amount of homework on weekends so that children have time for recreation and clubs. This does not mean reducing the requirements, but just redistributing the load to avoid burnout and keep the interest in learning. Many parents complain that kids are overloaded and don't have time to recover. - So you want to abolish weekend study altogether and turn school into a recreational club with no discipline or knowledge? Fine! Then let's remove exams, grades and textbooks, let children only play and relax. According to your logic, education is not necessary at all, because it is "tiring". You actually propose to raise a generation that cannot work and is incapable of intellectual work!
rationale: the school program should slightly reduce the amount of homework on weekends so that children have time for recreation and clubs[SEP]you want to abolish weekend study altogether and turn school into a recreational club with no discipline or knowledge

label: equivocation
text: I try to always keep my word, because my word is the basis of trust between people. If you have given your word, you have to keep it, otherwise you lose respect. A word can support, inspire, guide. Word has the power of influence, which means that every word I say already affects reality, so it's enough to say the right thing so that everything happens as it should. So actions are not as important as the right words.
rationale: Word has the power of influence, which means that every word I say already affects reality, so it's enough to say the right thing so that everything happens as it should.

label: false dilemma
text: International politics discusses participation in military alliances. The decision affects security and foreign relations. It is argued that: either we join a strong military alliance, receiving the protection and support of allies. This strengthens defense capabilities. rationale: either we join a strong military alliance,[SEP]Or we stand alone and become an easy target

rationale: Order creates clarity and helps you control what is going on.[SEP]Meaning that if I strictly follow the mathematical order, my life also becomes orderly and successful

text: When I was learning to play guitar, I was told that it is important to feel the harmony. Music becomes harmonious when all the sounds are in harmony. rationale: That's why we need to allow total wiretapping with no restrictions.

text: At a meeting on security, an official said: Terrorist threats are becoming more and more real, people are afraid for their loved ones. Do you really want to live in constant fear? It's destroying society. That's why we need to increase control and restrict freedoms for the sake of protection.

label: faulty generalization

You have to take into account competition, equipment costs, time to create content, and volatility of income. - A friend of mine started a YouTube channel and became a millionaire in a year. So all bloggers get rich! She made cooking videos - she just cooked at home and posted the videos. Six months later she had a million subscribers, brands were offering advertising themselves, and she bought an apartment in the center of the city. If she managed to get rich, then anyone else can do it. You don't need to listen to statistics about 95% - they are just losers who don't know how to work. Blogging is a gold mine for anyone who is willing to try. If one person became a millionaire, then everyone can do it.
rationale: A friend of mine started a YouTube channel and became a millionaire in a year. So all bloggers get rich! She made cooking videos - she just cooked at home and posted the videos. Six months later she had a million subscribers, brands were offering advertising themselves, and she bought an apartment in the center of the city. If she managed to get rich, then anyone else can do it. You don't need to listen to statistics about 95% - they are just losers who don't know how to work. Blogging is a gold mine for anyone who is willing to try. If one person became a millionaire, then everyone can do it.

label: equivocation
text: It is important for me to keep a balance between work and rest. When the balance is maintained, one feels harmonious and does not burn out. It helps to live a sustainable life. label: circular reasoning

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a discussion on nutrition, one person cited studies on the harms of excessive sugar consumption. This chef has won international awards, hosts popular shows and knows everything about food. His expertise in cooking is taken as proof that his views on health are correct. So scientific studies can be ignored - the opinion of a culinary authority seems more convincing.
rationale: sugar is not harmful, because a famous chef says you can eat it without restriction[SEP]If a recognized culinary master says so, it is true

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In a conversation about health, one person explains the importance of physical activity. He replies: This method of exercise is effective because it is recommended by a famous singer who keeps in great shape. This entertainer is known for his looks and popularity. If such a celebrity claims that his program works, then it really is the best. His success in music is taken as proof of competence in fitness. Even without scientific data, his opinion is considered sufficient.
rationale: This method of exercise is effective because it is recommended by a famous singer[SEP]If such a celebrity claims that his program works, then it really is the best

label: equivocation
text: I think about how important it is to look good, because appearance affects the perception of others. People judge by the way they dress, the way they look, the way they act. Appearance determines how information is perceived. If the look is right, then all the content is automatically perceived correctly. Therefore, it is enough to look convincing and any ideas will be accepted without any doubts.
rationale: Appearance determines how information is perceived. If the look is right, then all the content is automatically perceived correctly.

label: false dilemma
text: The debate over military spending is often characterized by categorical assessments. The state must provide security, but resources are limited. Either we substantially increase the military budget, even at the expense of cutting social programs, because security is declared the number one priority. Alternative strategies are not discussed. Either the country remains defenseless against external threats, because underfunding the military supposedly leads to vulnerability.
rationale: Either we substantially increase the military budget[SEP]Either the country remains defenseless against external threats

label: ad hominem

label: fallacy of credibility
text: In an environmental discussion, one participant presented data on air pollution. You have no specialized education, have not worked for environmental organizations, and have not published in scientific journals. Without that, you can't evaluate environmental problems. Even if the data looks convincing, lack of status makes it unreliable. You should only listen to recognized experts.
rationale: Your arguments have no weight[SEP]because you are not a certified environmentalist

label: equivocation
text: I am passionate about drawing and spend a lot of time practicing. A brush should be comfortable and accurate, otherwise the lines are uneven. A good brush helps to create beautiful works and control the process. The brush of the hand should also be developed and strong, because it determines the accuracy of movements in any work. So I believe that if you develop your brush, you can become a better artist, because everything depends on the brush.
rationale: brush of the hand should also be developed and strong, because it determines the accuracy of movements[SEP]if you develop your brush, you can become a better artist

text: - Universities record a drop in the level of applicants' preparation: 40% of first-year students fail basic mathematics, and teachers have to spend time repeating the school curriculum. text: One neighbor making noise at night means that all the residents of this house do not respect the rules. The management company received a complaint. No response yet.

label: equivocation
text: When I work in the workshop, I always keep a close eye on the tools. The key is especially important: without it, it is impossible to open complex mechanisms and get things working. A key opens access to important things, which means that if I have a key to understanding a subject, I can already open any problem. Therefore, knowing one subject gives me access to all knowledge. It is enough to find the right approach, and all doors in learning open by themselves.
rationale: A key opens access to important things, which means that if I have a key to understanding a subject, I can already open any problem. Therefore, knowing one subject gives me access to all knowledge.

label: ad populum
text: In a discussion of the startup, an investor remarked: Not a single major fund has invested in this project, so it's obviously a failure. The team tried to show the prototype.

label: equivocation
text: I think of depth of personality as an important quality. A deep person is able to understand complex things and see hidden meanings. This makes him interesting. label: intentional
text: The doctor justifies the experimental treatment: Yes, the method did not pass all clinical trials, but my intention was to save the patient at any cost. In a situation where standard methods do not work, alternatives should be sought. The risks are not so important when the goal is a person's life and the hope of recovery. Even if the result was negative, the choice itself was justified because it was well-meaning.
rationale: Yes, the method did not pass all clinical trials, but my intention was to save the patient at any cost.[SEP]The risks are not so important when the goal is a person's life and the hope of recovery.

label: false causality
text: When I started drinking more water throughout the day, I started getting good ideas for work more often, even in normal situations. It's a regular occurrence. So the amount of water you drink is directly related to your creativity.

label: ad populum
text: - Is this startup worth investing in? - It has already been backed by thousands of small investors on crowdfunding platforms, which means that the project is reliable. It's unlikely that so many people would invest without a reason.
rationale: It has already been backed by thousands of small investors on crowdfunding platforms, which means that the project is reliable.

text: - The number of accidents on public transport has increased in the city: 120 incidents, including serious injuries to passengers, were recorded during the year. Bicycles are environmentally friendly and modern, they are a trend all over the world.
rationale: Let's better discuss the development of bicycle lanes and popularization of sports, not the problems of public transport.
